g.data-narration: Welcome to NETCONF-YANG for IoT Management. This lesson covers modern protocols for configuring and managing billions of IoT devices securely.
IoT environments face unique challenges: billions of diverse devices, complex configurations, security risks, and need for automated management at scale.
NETCONF is a configuration protocol using XML. YANG models the data. Together they provide secure, standardized device management for IoT systems.
Compared to SNMP, NETCONF-YANG offers stronger security, rich data modeling, transaction support, and better suitability for IoT environments.
YANG provides five core data constructs: Leaf holds single values, Leaf-list holds sequences, Container groups nodes, List holds entries, and Choice selects alternatives.
A YANG module defines namespace, revision history, and data structures. The module example-iot-sensor shows containers with leaves for device configuration and state.
This YANG example shows leaf definitions with types like string, uint32, decimal64, and boolean. Each leaf can have units, defaults, and descriptions.
g.text: YANG Leaf Examples for IoT Device

g.data-narration: NETCONF operations include get-config for retrieving configs, edit-config for modifications, lock/unlock for concurrency, and get for state data.
g.text: Use get-config to retrieve, edit-config to modify, get for state data, lock prevents concurrent changes

g.data-narration: This NETCONF example shows an edit-config RPC to set sensor parameters. The client sends XML, server replies with ok or error.
The architecture shows Management Applications communicating via NETCONF Server to IoT Devices. YANG Models define data, Config Database stores configurations.
NETCONF-YANG offers standardization, transaction support, built-in validation, extensibility, strong security, and human-readable models for IoT.
In summary: YANG models configuration and state data with five constructs. NETCONF provides operations. Together they enable secure, automated IoT device management.
g.text: ✓ YANG: Data modeling language with leaf, leaf-list, container, list, choice constructs
✓ NETCONF: XML-based protocol with get, edit-config, lock operations
✓ Config vs State: config true for settings, config false for read-only operational data
✓ Replaces SNMP MIBs with human-readable YANG models
✓ Ideal for large-scale IoT with security and automation needs
✓ RESTCONF uses same YANG models over HTTP
